rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the 13100 affidavits did not include the full language required by Probate Code Section 13101(a)(5), and the death certificates attached to the affidavits appeared to be photocopies, not original, certified copies. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER ZIDAN KWAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/23/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "supplement" filed on 11/17/25 AMENDED the petition to include a request to withhold a reserve. (2) The $600 reserve was also not factored into the calculation of the proposed distribution. (3) Where in the accounting is the $332.84 "additional property received" shown, used in calculating the statutory compensation (paragraph 26 of the petition)? (4) Refunds for disbursements during estate administration are not income includable in the fee base. In the first accounting period, there were refunds totaling $532.82. In this accounting, there were refunds totaling $660.32. (5) On the other hand, the losses on sales used in the fee calculation appears to be incorrect (too high), so that, even with the reduced receipts, the statutory compensation should be higher... (6) The third amended creditor's claim, filed on 9/24/24, was for $1,741.11. Why was $1,849.79 paid? (7) The accounting period ends 8/3/25, but the incomplete and truncated bank statements precede, not encompass, that date. (8) The one page of the statement ending 7/31/25 shows a balance of $118,560.06, but the accounting shows $111,779.77. No reconciliation is shown. [LRSF 14.34 D.] (9) Was Notice of Proposed Action given before disposing of the tangible personal property? [Probate Code Section 10513.] (10) The address for Douglas Kwan is different on the proof of service from the address shown in the verified petition. (11) Did the administrator with will annexed or her attorney contact Collin Zidan Kwan to confirm his address? [CRC 7.51(a).] Or did he consent in writing to receiving notice in care of another? (12) The administrator with will annexed was authorized to pay the landlord tenant attorney a total of $5,041.00, but the accounting shows a total of $5,041.10 paid to him (without referencing any dates of the orders authorizing his fees). (13) Please file a copy of the seller's closing statement for each property to verify the costs of sale. (14) The attorney costs - in addition to not being in chronological order - include some entries that do not appear to be correct, and the vague descriptions are not helpful. There were numerous "status reports" filed in this matter, but none of the dates appears to correspond to the register of actions. The register of actions does not reflect any proof of service of notice of the 850 petition, either on or around 6/11/23 or 7/30/23. There was no need to file an ex parte petition to pay a creditor's claim. (15) All of the cash, exceeding the FDIC insured amount, is held at one financial institution. (16) A 13100 affidavit must have the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-300 attached to it. (17) If copies or printouts of bank statements are filed (attached to an e-filed petition), the fiduciary must verify that they are original. [CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts.] Please provide a revised proposed order that begins with "Order for Final Distribution" [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/9/25. Notice of the amendment must be served after the amendment is e-filed. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A reappraisal for sale or transfer of cash from one bank to another or an invoice from the attorney for expenses is not a change in the FORM of assets. The receipts for the blocked accounts were not on the mandatory Judicial Council form MC-356 with a copy of the order attached to it. A summary of account should show the schedules by letter or number, not just a description. "Disbursements and Payments" were actually on two separate schedules. Unless a beneficiary was reimbursed for costs advanced, an accounting should not have a schedule of "Disbursements to Beneficiaries.")

The I&A filed on November 30, 2023, shows a valuation of $1,000,000. 4) Eric M. Hass has requested special notice in this estate but was not given notice of the instant petition. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARNOLD YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/24/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Now, a second amended petition has been filed, but the caption still shows "amended" petition. (2) The notice of hearing does not have even this full incorrect caption. (3) The caption itself is contradictory: is there an accounting or waiver of accounting? (4) The executor, as the sole beneficiary, presumably waives an accounting by herself to herself. (5) The Summary of Account in paragraph 10 of the petition does not balance. (6) Refunds for disbursements made during estate administration are not receipts includable in the fee base. (7) Apportionment of the statutory compensation must be based on the agreement of the personal representatives or attorneys. [CRC 7.704(a).] (8) Payments to a third party to prepare the accounting must be deducted from the personal representative's statutory compensation. (9) As previously noted, Executor should pay the claims she has allowed before final distribution will be ordered. A petition for final distribution should not request to withhold the entire estate for such payments. (10) The amount proposed to be distributed to Yong Hong Fan (paragraph 33) does not appear to be correct. (11) Other than the unpaid creditors (who did not file requests for special notice in any event), who are the other people listed in paragraph 31? (12) The reserve shown on Schedule K appears to be excessive - and reflects taxes not yet paid for 2021 and 2022, although paragraph 20 states that all federal and state taxes have been paid or otherwise satisfied. (13) There should not be another $4,000 in filing fees payable to the Court. (14) There was no need to serve notice of proposed action; the executor is the sole beneficiary. Please provide a courtesy copy of an amendment to this [second] amended petition, showing that creditors have been paid, and a revised proposed order and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/10/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YUK YEE LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/30/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1. Paragraph 23 of the petition states the estate is solvent/insolvent. Please confirm that the estate is solvent. 2. The petition was not verified by the petitioner. An unverified pleading may be proved by filing a supplemental declaration that identifies the pleading by caption and its filing date [Local Rule 14.15C]. 3. Assets on hand must be specified, including total cash and specific securities. The proposed distribution must also be specified after fees, costs, and reserve. Calculation of the proposed distribution of cash must be provided. 4. Proposed order must be filed again after issues addressed above. The caption of the order must also be complete (include language re: fees and costs).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSIE NELL TURNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amended petition requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) No proof of service has been filed. (3) Notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) No proof of publication of the Notice of Amended Petition to Administer Estate has been filed. (5) Item 1 of the amended petition is still incomplete. (6) Item 2d of the amended petition is incomplete. (7) The heirs have not waived bond. (8) If the decedent resided and died in Vallejo, how is San Francisco the proper venue? (9) The children, who are the heirs, have priority before a grandchild. They must either decline to serve or nominate the petitioner. (10) Items 6 and 7 do not need to be completed if a decedent is survived by issue. If this is the proper venue, and the amended petition is not dismissed, please provide courtesy copies of the any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/9/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBERT L. POWELL, JR. REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Trust'S Title To Personal Property(Heggstad Petition)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The incorrect form of notice was served, DE-120 instead of DE-115. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The successor trustee resides in Georgia. [Probate Code Section 17002(a).] How is this the proper venue? (4) Courtesy copies were not provided, so the petition was skimmed online. It does not appear that a copy of the Wells Fargo savings account xx8990 was attached to the petition. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/7/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
